root_text.an4.196:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ.
root_text.an4.196:1.2: Atha kho sāḷho ca licchavi abhayo ca licchavi yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho sāḷho licchavi bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an4.196:2.1: “Santi, bhante, eke samaṇabrāhmaṇā dvayena oghassa nittharaṇaṁ paññapenti—
root_text.an4.196:3.1: “Sīlavisuddhiṁ kho ahaṁ, sāḷha, aññataraṁ sāmaññaṅganti vadāmi.
root_text.an4.196:3.2: Ye te, sāḷha, samaṇabrāhmaṇā tapojigucchāvādā tapojigucchāsārā tapojigucchāallīnā viharanti, abhabbā te oghassa nittharaṇāya.
root_text.an4.196:3.3: Yepi te, sāḷha, samaṇabrāhmaṇā aparisuddhakāyasamācārā aparisuddhavacīsamācārā aparisuddhamanosamācārā aparisuddhājīvā, abhabbā te ñāṇadassanāya anuttarāya sambodhāya.
root_text.an4.196:4.1: Seyyathāpi, sāḷha, puriso nadiṁ taritukāmo tiṇhaṁ kuṭhāriṁ ādāya vanaṁ paviseyya.
root_text.an4.196:4.2: So tattha passeyya mahatiṁ sālalaṭṭhiṁ ujuṁ navaṁ akukkuccakajātaṁ.
root_text.an4.196:5.2: bhabbo nu kho so puriso nadiṁ taritun”ti?
root_text.an4.196:5.5: “Asu hi, bhante, sālalaṭṭhi bahiddhā suparikammakatā anto avisuddhā.
root_text.an4.196:5.6: Tassetaṁ pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—sālalaṭṭhi saṁsīdissati, puriso anayabyasanaṁ āpajjissatī”ti.
root_text.an4.196:6.1: “Evamevaṁ kho, sāḷha, ye te samaṇabrāhmaṇā tapojigucchāvādā tapojigucchāsārā tapojigucchāallīnā viharanti, abhabbā te oghassa nittharaṇāya.
root_text.an4.196:6.2: Yepi te, sāḷha, samaṇabrāhmaṇā aparisuddhakāyasamācārā aparisuddhavacīsamācārā aparisuddhamanosamācārā aparisuddhājīvā, abhabbā te ñāṇadassanāya anuttarāya sambodhāya.
root_text.an4.196:7.1: Ye ca kho te, sāḷha, samaṇabrāhmaṇā na tapojigucchāvādā na tapojigucchāsārā na tapojigucchāallīnā viharanti, bhabbā te oghassa nittharaṇāya.
root_text.an4.196:7.2: Yepi te, sāḷha, samaṇabrāhmaṇā parisuddhakāyasamācārā parisuddhavacīsamācārā parisuddhamanosamācārā parisuddhājīvā, bhabbā te ñāṇadassanāya anuttarāya sambodhāya.
root_text.an4.196:8.1: Seyyathāpi, sāḷha, puriso nadiṁ taritukāmo tiṇhaṁ kuṭhāriṁ ādāya vanaṁ paviseyya.
root_text.an4.196:8.2: So tattha passeyya mahatiṁ sālalaṭṭhiṁ ujuṁ navaṁ akukkuccakajātaṁ.
root_text.an4.196:8.8: vāsīhi tacchetvā nikhādanaṁ ādāya anto suvisodhitaṁ visodheyya;
root_text.an4.196:9.2: bhabbo nu kho so puriso nadiṁ taritun”ti?
root_text.an4.196:9.5: “Asu hi, bhante, sālalaṭṭhi bahiddhā suparikammakatā, anto suvisuddhā nāvākatā phiyārittabaddhā.
root_text.an4.196:9.7: ‘nāvā na saṁsīdissati, puriso sotthinā pāraṁ gamissatī’”ti.
root_text.an4.196:10.1: “Evamevaṁ kho, sāḷha, ye te samaṇabrāhmaṇā na tapojigucchāvādā na tapojigucchāsārā na tapojigucchāallīnā viharanti, bhabbā te oghassa nittharaṇāya.
root_text.an4.196:10.2: Yepi te, sāḷha, samaṇabrāhmaṇā parisuddhakāyasamācārā parisuddhavacīsamācārā parisuddhamanosamācārā parisuddhājīvā, bhabbā te ñāṇadassanāya anuttarāya sambodhāya.
root_text.an4.196:10.3: Seyyathāpi, sāḷha, yodhājīvo bahūni cepi kaṇḍacitrakāni jānāti;
root_text.an4.196:10.4: atha kho so tīhi ṭhānehi rājāraho hoti rājabhoggo, rañño aṅganteva saṅkhaṁ gacchati.
root_text.an4.196:10.6: Dūrepātī ca, akkhaṇavedhī ca, mahato ca kāyassa padāletā.
root_text.an4.196:11.2: evamevaṁ kho, sāḷha, ariyasāvako sammāsamādhi hoti.
root_text.an4.196:11.3: Sammāsamādhi, sāḷha, ariyasāvako yaṁ kiñci rūpaṁ atītānāgatapaccuppannaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā oḷārikaṁ vā sukhumaṁ vā hīnaṁ vā paṇītaṁ vā yaṁ dūre santike vā sabbaṁ rūpaṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya passati.
root_text.an4.196:11.7: yaṁ kiñci viññāṇaṁ atītānāgatapaccuppannaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā oḷārikaṁ vā sukhumaṁ vā hīnaṁ vā paṇītaṁ vā yaṁ dūre santike vā, sabbaṁ viññāṇaṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya passati.
root_text.an4.196:12.2: evamevaṁ kho, sāḷha, ariyasāvako sammādiṭṭhi hoti.
root_text.an4.196:12.3: Sammādiṭṭhi, sāḷha, ariyasāvako ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an4.196:13.1: Seyyathāpi, sāḷha, yodhājīvo mahato kāyassa padāletā;
root_text.an4.196:13.2: evamevaṁ kho, sāḷha, ariyasāvako sammāvimutti hoti.
root_text.an4.196:13.3: Sammāvimutti, sāḷha, ariyasāvako mahantaṁ avijjākkhandhaṁ padāletī”ti.

translation_text.an4.196:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, at the Great Wood, in the hall with the peaked roof.
translation_text.an4.196:1.2: Then Sāḷha and Abhaya the Licchavis went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an4.196:2.1: “There are, sir, some ascetics and brahmins who advocate crossing the flood by means of two things:
translation_text.an4.196:2.2: purification of ethics, and mortification in disgust of sin.
translation_text.an4.196:2.3: What does the Buddha say about this?”
translation_text.an4.196:3.1: “Sāḷha, purification of ethics is one of the factors of the ascetic life, I say.
translation_text.an4.196:3.2: But those ascetics and brahmins who teach fervent mortification in disgust of sin—regarding it as essential and sticking to it—are incapable of crossing the flood.
translation_text.an4.196:3.3: And those ascetics and brahmins whose livelihood and behavior by way of body, speech, and mind, is not pure are also incapable of knowing and seeing, of supreme awakening.
translation_text.an4.196:4.1: Suppose a man who wanted to cross a river took a sharp axe into a wood.
translation_text.an4.196:4.2: There he’d see a large green sal tree, straight and young and grown unskewed.
translation_text.an4.196:4.3: He’d cut it down at the base,
translation_text.an4.196:4.5: and completely strip off the branches and foliage.
translation_text.an4.196:4.6: Then he’d trim it with axes and
translation_text.an4.196:4.9: and sand it with a rock.
translation_text.an4.196:4.10: Finally, he’d launch out on the river.
translation_text.an4.196:5.2: Is that man capable of crossing the river?”
translation_text.an4.196:5.5: Because that green sal tree is well worked on the outside, but inside it’s still not cleared out.
translation_text.an4.196:5.6: I’d expect that green sal tree to sink, and the man to come to ruin.”
translation_text.an4.196:6.1: “In the same way, Sāḷha, those ascetics and brahmins who teach fervent mortification in disgust of sin—regarding it as essential and sticking to it—are incapable of crossing the flood.
translation_text.an4.196:6.2: And those ascetics and brahmins whose livelihood and behavior by way of body, speech, and mind is not pure are also incapable of knowing and seeing, of supreme awakening.
translation_text.an4.196:7.1: But those ascetics and brahmins who don’t teach mortification in disgust of sin—not regarding it as essential or sticking to it—are capable of crossing the flood.
translation_text.an4.196:7.2: And those ascetics and brahmins whose behavior by way of body, speech, and mind is pure are also capable of knowing and seeing, of supreme awakening.
translation_text.an4.196:8.1: Suppose a man who wanted to cross a river took a sharp axe into a wood.
translation_text.an4.196:8.2: There he’d see a large green sal tree, straight and young and grown unskewed.
translation_text.an4.196:8.3: He’d cut it down at the base,
translation_text.an4.196:8.5: and completely strip off the branches and foliage.
translation_text.an4.196:8.6: Then he’d trim it with axes and
translation_text.an4.196:8.8: Then he’d take a chisel and completely clear it out inside.
translation_text.an4.196:8.11: and make it into a boat.
translation_text.an4.196:8.12: Finally he’d fix it with oars and rudder,
translation_text.an4.196:8.13: and launch out on the river.
translation_text.an4.196:9.2: Is that man capable of crossing the river?”
translation_text.an4.196:9.5: Because that green sal tree is well worked on the outside, cleared out on the inside, made into a boat, and fixed with oars and rudder.
translation_text.an4.196:9.7: that boat to not sink, and the man to safely make it to the far shore.”
translation_text.an4.196:10.1: “In the same way, Sāḷha, those ascetics and brahmins who don’t teach fervent mortification in disgust of sin—not regarding it as essential or sticking to it—are capable of crossing the flood.
translation_text.an4.196:10.2: And those ascetics and brahmins whose behavior by way of body, speech, and mind is pure are also capable of knowing and seeing, of supreme awakening.
translation_text.an4.196:10.3: Suppose there was a warrior who knew lots of fancy archery tricks.
translation_text.an4.196:10.4: It is only with these three factors that he becomes worthy of a king, fit to serve a king, and is reckoned a factor of kingship.
translation_text.an4.196:10.6: He’s a long-distance shooter, a marksman, and one who shatters large objects.
translation_text.an4.196:11.1: Just as a warrior is a long-distance shooter,
translation_text.an4.196:11.2: a noble disciple has right immersion.
translation_text.an4.196:11.3: A noble disciple with right immersion truly sees any kind of form at all—past, future, or present; internal or external; solid or subtle; inferior or superior; far or near: all form—with right understanding: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.an4.196:11.4: They truly see any kind of feeling …
translation_text.an4.196:11.7: consciousness at all—past, future, or present; internal or external; solid or subtle; inferior or superior; far or near, all consciousness—with right understanding: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.an4.196:12.1: Just as a warrior is a marksman,
translation_text.an4.196:12.2: a noble disciple has right view.
translation_text.an4.196:12.3: A noble disciple with right view truly understands: ‘This is suffering’ … ‘This is the origin of suffering’ … ‘This is the cessation of suffering’ … ‘This is the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering’.
translation_text.an4.196:13.1: Just as a warrior shatters large objects,
translation_text.an4.196:13.2: a noble disciple has right freedom.
translation_text.an4.196:13.3: A noble disciple with right freedom shatters the great mass of ignorance.”
